depression.description: I often feel like I'm unlovable and that no one truly cares about me. This belief makes it hard for me to connect with others.
Whenever I encounter authority figures, I feel this overwhelming frustration because I believe they can't be trusted to treat me fairly.
I get so angry when I think about situations where I feel I've been treated unfairly, especially at work. It consumes my thoughts and affects how I interact with others.
I constantly worry that people will leave me or abandon me, which makes it hard for me to open up and trust anyone.
I often feel like my feelings and reactions aren't valid, which makes me question myself and my responses to situations.

Delusion.description: I believe that most people are fundamentally selfish and will exploit me if given the chance. This makes it hard for me to rely on anyone.
I find myself seeking validation by arguing my points, but I often feel like I'm just trying to prove that I'm right rather than connecting with others.
